title: Setting capabilities is security-sensitive
description: Setting capabilities is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalsecuritySecurity HotspotcweSetting capabilities can lead to privilege escalation and container escapes.Linux capabilities allow you to assign narrow slices ofroot's permissions to processes. A thread with capabilities bypasses the normal kernel security checks to execute high-privilege actions such as mounting a device to a directory, without requiring additional root privileges.In a container, capabilities might allow to access resources from the host system which can result in container escapes. For example, with the capabilitySYS_ADMINan attacker might be able to mount devices from the host system inside of the container.Ask Yourself WhetherCapabilities are granted:To a process that does not require all c
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: SYS_ADMIN capability detected - can lead to privilege escalation and container escape
SYS_MODULE capability detected - allows loading kernel modules (high security risk)
SYS_PTRACE capability detected - allows process tracing (security sensitive)
DAC_OVERRIDE capability detected - bypasses file permission checks (security risk)
SETUID/SETGID capabilities detected - allow UID/GID manipulation (privilege escalation risk)
SYS_RAWIO capability detected - provides raw I/O access (security sensitive)
NET_RAW capability detected - allows network packet sniffing (security risk)
NET_ADMIN capability detected - allows network configuration changes (security sensitive)
SYS_CHROOT capability detected - allows changing root directory (potential escape vector)
SYS_TIME capability detected - allows system time modification (security sensitive)
ALL capabilities granted - extremely dangerous, use minimal required capabilities only
SYS_NICE capability detected - allows process priority manipulation (review necessity)
IPC_LOCK capability detected - allows memory locking (review if necessary)
MKNOD capability detected - allows device file creation (security sensitive)
NET_BIND_SERVICE capability detected - review if binding to privileged ports is necessary
